steps.title: Set up the confidence interval
steps.content: Point estimate 8.5%, margin of error 2.73%; the plausible range extends one margin of error in each direction from the estimate.

steps.title: Compute the bounds
steps.content: Lower: 8.5 − 2.73 = 5.77%. Upper: 8.5 + 2.73 = 11.23%. Any value in [5.77%, 11.23%] is plausible for the true defect rate.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• A: 2.73% of the items are defective — 2.73% is the margin of error, not a defect rate estimate. Eliminated.
  • B: It is plausible that between 5.77% and 11.23% of the items are defective — This exactly describes the confidence interval derived above. That matches choice B — B is correct.
  • C: 8.5% of the items are defective — This treats the estimate as exact, ignoring the margin of error entirely. Eliminated.
  • D: It is plausible that more than 11.23% are defective — 11.23% is the upper bound; values above it are not plausible given the interval. Eliminated.
